original_hypothesis.title: RollupAdminLogic setBaseStake and setMinimumAssertionPeriod Allow Zero Values Disabling Economic Security
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: RollupAdminLogic.setBaseStake has no minimum value check and can set baseStake to 0, eliminating the economic security requirement for assertion creation. Similarly, setMinimumAssertionPeriod can be set to 0, allowing assertions to be created with no time gap. Combined, these enable zero-cost assertion spam. In contrast, setConfirmPeriodBlocks correctly enforces > 0. The admin pathway relies on proxy routing for access control, so if governance is compromised, the protocol's economic security can be instantly disabled.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Missing input validation on critical economic parameters could allow a compromised admin to instantly disable economic security. The asymmetric validation (setConfirmPeriodBlocks checks > 0, but setBaseStake and setMinimumAssertionPeriod do not) suggests incomplete parameter validation.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setBaseStake(uint256 newBaseStake) has no validation; setMinimumAssertionPeriod(uint256 newPeriod) has no validation; contrast with setConfirmPeriodBlocks which requires newConfirmPeriod > 0

preconditions: Admin (proxy admin / upgrade executor) calls setBaseStake(0) or setMinimumAssertionPeriod(0)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: setBaseStake(0) allows zero-cost assertion creation; setMinimumAssertionPeriod(0) allows unbounded assertion frequency

operational_questions: Is there governance-layer validation preventing zero baseStake?
What are the current production values for these parameters?
Does the user logic have additional checks on these values?
validation_requirements: Test assertion creation rate with minimumAssertionPeriod=0

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Compromise admin key or governance mechanism
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Create assertions with zero stake at unlimited rate
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Flood the assertion tree, potentially confirming malicious state

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Zero-cost assertion spam; potential for malicious state confirmation
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Admin can restore values, but damage from confirmed malicious assertions may be irreversible
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Assertion creation, staking, protocol economic security

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: setBaseStake or setMinimumAssertionPeriod events with zero values
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Admin function calls reducing security parameters

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
